FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Struct TransitionDynamics

Namespace
FluentGpu.Foundation
Assembly
FluentGpu.Foundation.dll

The dynamics of a transition. Springs are the default (interruptible, jump-free retarget): Response ≈ the settle time in seconds, DampingRatio 1 = critical (no overshoot), <1 = bouncy. A tween is opt-in: fixed DurationMs + Easing (an EasingSpec, so authored WinUI KeySplines are expressible; a default spec is normalized by the engine to FluentDecelerate). Stored as scalars (not the AnimEngine's SpringParams) so the spec can live in Foundation; the engine converts at seed time. A default value (all zero) is normalized by the engine to the spring defaults.

public readonly record struct TransitionDynamics : IEquatable<TransitionDynamics>
Implements
IEquatable<TransitionDynamics>
Inherited Members

ValueType.GetHashCode()
ValueType.ToString()

Constructors

TransitionDynamics(DynamicsKind, float, float, float, EasingSpec)

The dynamics of a transition. Springs are the default (interruptible, jump-free retarget): Response ≈ the settle time in seconds, DampingRatio 1 = critical (no overshoot), <1 = bouncy. A tween is opt-in: fixed DurationMs + Easing (an EasingSpec, so authored WinUI KeySplines are expressible; a default spec is normalized by the engine to FluentDecelerate). Stored as scalars (not the AnimEngine's SpringParams) so the spec can live in Foundation; the engine converts at seed time. A default value (all zero) is normalized by the engine to the spring defaults.

public TransitionDynamics(DynamicsKind Kind = DynamicsKind.Spring, float Response = 0.3, float DampingRatio = 0.85, float DurationMs = 0, EasingSpec Easing = default)

Response float
DampingRatio float
DurationMs float
Easing EasingSpec

TransitionDynamics
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
